title: Media Hutch (Basic Collection)
summary: A wide open hutch with two center dividers splitting three cubbies, each closed by its own hinged door, capped with nosing trim along the top edge.
description:
A single 1x12 box — two sides, a top, and a shelf — gets split into three equal cubbies by two full-height dividers, with 1x2 trim capping the top edge of each opening. **The plywood back closes the whole hutch in**, and the back frame pieces reinforce it against the box before the doors go on.

Each of the three cubbies gets its own hinged door, cut from thinner 1/2" stock (or a standard 3/4" board if that's what's on hand) — self-closing surface-mount overlay hinges keep the doors flush without needing a rabbet or inset fit, which is what makes this a genuinely approachable door installation even without cabinet-building experience.

**A strip of 3/4" edge nosing wraps the top front face** for a finished, rounded-over look instead of a square plywood edge — this small trim detail is what elevates the piece from a plain box to something that reads as furniture.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, or 2" self-tapping screws

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Assemble the sides, top, and shelf into a single wide box.
materials: Pocket hole screws, or 2" self-tapping screws

steps.title: Add the dividers and trim
steps.body: Install the two cubby dividers and the top trim above each opening.

steps.title: Add the back frame and panel
steps.body: Attach the back frame and support, then nail on the plywood back.

steps.title: Add the top edge nosing
steps.body: Attach the edge nosing strip across the top front face.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Cut the three door faces and hang each with self-closing surface mount overlay hinges.

steps.title: Add hardware and finish
steps.body: Attach pulls or knobs, sand, and apply finishing supplies.

unresolvedImages.alt: Media hutch with three hinged cubby doors
